sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_time DATETIME NOT NULL, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_time, description) VALUES (1, current_timestamp, 'Event 1');
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_time, description) VALUES (2, current_timestamp - INTERVAL '1' DAY, 'Event 2');
SELECT id, event_time, description FROM test_dates WHERE event_time = current_timestamp OR event_time IS NULL;
SELECT id, event_time, description FROM test_dates WHERE (current_timestamp OR event_time) BETWEEN current_timestamp - INTERVAL '2' DAY AND current_timestamp;
SELECT id, event_time, description FROM test_dates WHERE NOT (event_time IS NULL OR event_time = current_timestamp);
SELECT id, event_time, description FROM test_dates WHERE event_time = (SELECT MAX(event_time) FROM test_dates) OR event_time < current_timestamp;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER () AS cnt_events FROM test_dates WHERE event_time >= current_timestamp OR description LIKE '%Event%';
SELECT id, event_time FROM test_dates GROUP BY event_time HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 OR current_timestamp > MIN(event_time);
CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, str_value VARCHAR(255));
SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE str_value = CAST(current_timestamp AS VARCHAR) OR LENGTH(str_value) = 0;
SELECT id FROM test_dates WHERE (event_time = current_timestamp OR description IS NOT NULL) AND (event_time IS NOT NULL OR id = 1);
